title: Evidence Synthesis, Claims, and Contradiction Review Policy
principles: Claims remain separate from evidence records and source documents.
Supporting, qualifying, conflicting, and contextual evidence remain explicitly typed.
Public claims and syntheses require human approval.
Contradictions and unresolved uncertainty remain visible rather than being averaged away.
Deterministic synthesis may organize evidence but cannot invent sources or citations.
AI-assisted wording must remain grounded in registered evidence IDs and visibly labeled.
boundaries: No fabricated evidence, quotations, citations, or source authority.
No automatic legal, medical, scientific, security, or policy determination.
No causal conclusion from correlation, proximity, sequence, or majority count alone.
No public synthesis without human review and an approved claim.
No suppression of conflicting evidence or unresolved uncertainty.
No individual targeting, surveillance, risk scoring, or consequential automated action.
